item.title: Iranian protesters who escaped tell CBS News war has made things worse
item.description: Two Iranian brothers who joined January's protests say the war has made things worse, and ending it shouldn't only be about uranium.

item.title: Trump threatens to bomb Oman if they don't "behave" in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump threatened to bomb Oman, a key American ally, if they don't "behave" in the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata reports.

item.title: $90,000 CD vs. $90,000 high-yield savings account vs. $90,000 money market account: Here's which will earn more in one year
item.description: Not sure if a $90,000 deposit is worth making into one of these three account types? Here's what to consider now.

item.title: Supreme Court sides with Black death row inmate in jury selection racial discrimination case
item.description: The Supreme Court has ruled in favor of a Black death row inmate who argued racial discrimination occurred in the jury-selection process before his trial. CBS News' Katrina Kaufman reports.

item.title: Iranian protesters who fled Iran say war has made the regime "even more extreme"
item.description: Two Iranian brothers who joined the January protests told CBS News' Imtiaz Tyab the situation inside Iran "became chaotic" after the war started and continued to deteriorate. "After the ceasefire, the regime became even more extreme to the people," one of the brothers said. They fled Iran on May 13.

item.title: Can your Social Security be garnished after age 70?
item.description: Many retirees assume their benefits are fully protected, but the reality is often more complicated.

item.title: DOJ investigating whether Trump accuser E. Jean Carroll committed perjury
item.description: The Justice Department is conducting a criminal investigation into whether author E. Jean Carroll committed perjury in connection with her civil lawsuits against President Trump, sources familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Supreme Court sides with Black death row inmate who alleged discrimination
item.description: The Supreme Court divided 5-4, with Chief Justice John Roberts and Justice Brett Kavanaugh joining the three liberal justices in the majority.

item.title: Broadway star Joshua Henry says "Ragtime" musical is a "mirror of who we are"
item.description: Broadway star Joshua Henry joins "CBS Mornings" to discuss his role in the "Ragtime" musical, which depicts the stories of Americans at the turn of the 20th century.

item.title: Jill Biden says she was "frightened" after debate. Here's what she said at the time.
item.description: Jill Biden told CBS News "Sunday Morning" that her husband's performance in the 2024 debate "scared me to death." But her comments at the time did not reflect those concerns.

item.title: How the U.S. is preparing for the World Cup's security challenges like drones and terrorism
item.description: U.S. officials are preparing for thousands of visitors at sites linked to the FIFA World Cup. CBS News' Anna Schecter breaks down the measures.

item.title: Iranian-American journalist reacts to man's sentence for plotting her murder
item.description: Iranian-American journalist Masih Alinejad, a CBS News contributor, is reacting to the New York sentence for a man accused of plotting her assassination.

item.title: Jill Biden says husband "was slowing down" but wasn't in cognitive decline
item.description: Jill Biden said she never saw signs her husband, former President Joe Biden, was in cognitive decline, in an interview for "CBS Sunday Morning" airing May 31.

item.title: Nightmare supply chain issues at Strait of Hormuz as Iran war roars on
item.description: The global supply chain issues at the Strait of Hormuz continue as the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran continues. Nick Vyas, a founding member of the Kendrick Global Supply Chain Institute at the University of Southern California Marshall School of Business, joins with more insight.

item.title: Jill Biden denies Joe Biden was in cognitive decline, but acknowledges "he was slowing down"
item.description: Former first lady Jill Biden denied she saw signs her husband Joe Biden was in cognitive decline. "He was the same, the essence of the same Joe Biden, but yeah, he was slowing down," Biden said in an interview with "Sunday Morning" correspondent Rita Braver, airing Sunday, May 31.

item.title: Iran fires missile at Kuwait after second round of U.S. strikes in days
item.description: Iran says it targeted a U.S. base in retaliation for the second set of what the Pentagon calls "purely defensive" strikes carried out this week.

item.title: U.K. spy chief says nearly 500,000 Russian soldiers have died in Ukraine war
item.description: A U.K. spy chief says nearly 500,000 Russian soldiers have died in the war with Ukraine. CBS News' Ramy Inocencio reports.

item.title: Latest news on Iran war after Trump threatens to bomb Oman, a key U.S. ally
item.description: President Trump issued a stern warning against Oman, a key U.S. ally in the Middle East, amid high tensions in the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Aaron Navarro report.

item.title: What will happen to gold and silver prices this June? Here's what experts expect.
item.description: Gold and silver prices have changed noticeably in 2026. Here's where some experts expect them to head this June.

item.title: Blue micromoon makes rare appearance this weekend
item.description: A rare blue micromoon will appear in night skies this weekend. Here's what to expect.

item.title: U.S. setting up Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya, officials say
item.description: The U.S. is in the process of setting up a quarantine facility in Kenya for Americans who were exposed to Ebola​ or infected with the deadly illness.

item.title: First inflation report under new Fed chief shows prices still rising
item.description: The personal consumption expenditures price index, the Federal Reserve's preferred gauge of inflation, jumped due to higher energy costs.

item.title: The Trump Accounts app launches today. Here's what to know.
item.description: The Trump Accounts app allows parents to open new tax-preferred investment accounts for their children, including a $1,000 government contribution.

item.title: 3M sued for $1.4 billion by Australia over "forever chemicals" at defense bases
item.description: Australia is suing 3M for more than 2 billion Australian dollars ($1.4 billion) over so-called "forever chemical" contamination from firefighting foam at defense bases, the government says.

item.title: Google employee accused of making over $1.2M by insider trading on Polymarket
item.description: A software engineer at Google is facing federal charges after allegedly betting on confidential company information on Polymarket, netting more than $1.2 million in profits.

item.title: New York City man sentenced to 10 years in plot to kill Iranian American writer
item.description: A federal judge has given a onetime truck and bus driver charged in an assassination plot against an Iranian American writer a 10-year prison sentence.

item.title: High gas prices are forcing Southern California agencies to cut fuel spending
item.description: Since the start of the war with Iran in late February, through April, Pasadena's fire department has seen a 39% increase in fuel spending. During the same period, the police department also saw a 38% spike in fuel spending.

item.title: Jill Biden: "I was frightened" by 2024 debate, thought husband was having a stroke
item.description: Former first lady Jill Biden said she thought her husband, Joe Biden, was having a stroke during the 2024 debate against Donald Trump.

item.title: Trump holds Cabinet meeting as negotiations continue to end Iran war
item.description: President Trump held a Cabinet meeting at the White House after moving it from Camp David due to weather.

item.title: 1,500 strangers attend WWII veteran's funeral
item.description: Veterans service officer Terrance O'Keefe put out a call from Massachusetts for a World War II veteran who needed a proper hero's send-off. When he arrived at the funeral, a line was already out the church door.
